result:
Done. I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs`, and the project builds with zero warnings/errors.

- **`EscapeField`** — returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** — escapes each field and joins with commas; an empty list yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow`** — single-pass parser: a field starting with `"` is consumed until the closing un-doubled quote (with `""` contributing one literal quote, and commas/CR/LF inside kept as content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. The loop structure naturally makes the empty line parse to one empty field and a trailing comma produce a final empty field.

I verified behavior end-to-end with a throwaway console project in a temp directory (not in the source tree): escaping cases (plain, empty, comma, embedded quotes), `JoinRow([])`, empty-line and trailing-comma parses, and `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields)) == fields` round-trips over lists including quotes, CR/LF, commas, and empty fields — all passed. `NeedsQuoting` was left untouched. Two build cycles used out of the budget of ten.
